description_long: This is a black and white Ford Crown Victoria-style police car, featuring a classic two-tone paint scheme with black on the hood, trunk, and lower body, and white on the doors and roof. The roof displays a large black '507' number, flanked by a red and blue light bar at the center. The doors are marked with the word 'POLICE', the city seal, and additional text indicating the police department. The car is equipped with black wheels, a push bumper on the front, and visible antennas on the roof and trunk. The windows are tinted, and the vehicle has standard sedan proportions with four doors and a rear license plate. The overall appearance is authoritative and instantly recognizable as a law enforcement vehicle.
description: A black and white police sedan with prominent '507' roof numbering, police markings, and a red-blue light bar on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the push bumper, black hood, and the '507' number on the roof with the light bar visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the two-tone paint, police insignia on the door, and the full length of the vehicle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the trunk, rear license plate, and the '507' roof number, with antennas visible.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the police markings, light bar, and black wheels.
attribute_police_markings: Large black '507' number on the roof, 'POLICE' text and city seal on the front doors, and additional department text in black on the white door panels. The markings are bold and clear, with the number on the roof for aerial identification.
attribute_emergency_lights: A roof-mounted light bar with red and blue segments, centrally placed on the roof. The light bar is rectangular and clearly visible from all sides.
attribute_police_insignia: A round city seal insignia is visible on the front doors, featuring a multicolored design typical of municipal police emblems.
attribute_license_plate: A standard license plate is present on the front and rear bumpers, white with black text, and includes a code or number, but no special police markings are visible.
